events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate sequence check for water heater service call. Scenario: gas flex connector through cabinet wall. Work in progress. Need determine sequence violation and what must happen before work can continue. Need apply IPC 504, IRC P2804. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Observed field condition: gas flex connector through cabinet wall. This is likely non-compliant: gas appliance connectors (flex) cannot pass through walls, floors, partitions, or cabinets? Need recall codes. IPC 504? International Plumbing Code? Actually IPC 504 maybe water heater installation? IRC P2804 water heater seismic? Hmm. Gas flex connector through cabinet wall is a defect. Need sequence: before continuing, must remove/replace with proper rigid gas piping, proper shutoff, support, etc. Need identify component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Risk high? Gas leak/fire. Decision fail. Work stage in-progress. percent_complete maybe 70? value not asked 0. sound_source etc default. order empty? The task asks check order of work and catch sequence violation. We can include order? Not audio. Maybe order list of step IDs? Not provided. Could leave empty. workable maybe empty because work cannot continue until corrected. findings: gas appliance connector run through cabinet wall, prohibited; concealed/penetration; potential abrasion/leak. actions: stop work, shut off gas, remove flex connector, install approved rigid black iron/CSST through wall with proper sleeve/protection, shutoff valve within 6 ft, leak test, inspect. references: IPC 504.13? IRC P2804.2? Need be accurate. Let's recall exact code sections.

IPC 504: Installation of water heaters. 504.1 General, 504.2 Anchorage, 504.3 Location, 504.4 Prohibited locations, 504.5 Water heater safety devices, 504.6 Requirements for discharge piping, 504.7 Vacuum relief valve, 504.8 Pan, 504.9 Relief valve. Not gas piping. But scenario says reference IPC 504, IRC P2804. IRC P2804: Water heaters. P2804.1 General, P2804.2 Anchorage/seismic? P2804.3 Drain pan, P2804.4 Temperature/pressure relief valve, P2804.5 Discharge piping, P2804.6 Vacuum relief. Hmm. Gas flex connector through cabinet wall maybe under fuel gas code IFGC/IRC G2420, not IPC/IRC P2804. But prompt says apply IPC 504, IRC P2804. Maybe IPC 504.11? Let's search memory.

IPC 504.11 maybe "Fuel gas piping" not. Actually IPC includes Chapter 5 Water Heaters. 504.10 maybe "Fuel-fired water heaters" requiring venting. 504.11 maybe "Gas shutoff valves". Let me recall IPC 2018: Section 504 Water Heaters. 504.1 General. 504.2 Water heater anchorage. 504.3 Location. 504.4 Prohibited locations. 504.5 Water heater safety devices. 504.6 Requirements for discharge piping. 504.7 Vacuum relief valve. 504.8 Water heater drain pan. 504.9 Relief valve. 504.10 Access. 504.11 Fuel-burning water heaters maybe? Hmm.

IRC P2804: Section P2804 Water Heaters. P2804.1 General. P2804.2 Anchorage. P2804.3 Drain pan. P2804.4 Temperature and pressure relief valves. P2804.5 Discharge piping. P2804.6 Vacuum relief. P2804.7 Water heater pans. P2804.8 Prohibited locations. P2804.9 Fuel-burning water heaters maybe.

Gas flex connector through cabinet wall is likely prohibited by fuel gas code (IFGC 404.2? IRC G2422.1.2?). But prompt restricts to IPC 504, IRC P2804. Maybe there is a provision in IRC P2804 about gas connectors? P2804.2? Actually P2804.2 Anchorage and seismic bracing. Not relevant. Maybe the scenario is about water heater installation sequence: gas flex connector through cabinet wall before completing? The code references may be red herrings or need apply generally: IPC 504/IRC P2804 require water heaters installed per manufacturer's instructions and code; gas connectors through walls violate IFGC. But we only have those references. We can still cite them for general installation compliance and mention gas connector standards.

Need produce JSON. Let's think about fields:

decision: "fail"
risk: "high" or "critical"? Gas f…
